tolerance.notes: PPM coefficients are pure rationals (-1/12, 7/12); the parabolic reconstruction adds only multiplications and additions on top. Bit-for-bit agreement across bindings is impractical because of summation order, but the analytical-cell-average MMS pipeline is deterministic enough that the per-grid Linf error matches across bindings to ~1e-13 of the Julia reference.

reference_binding_notes: ESD ships a Julia evaluator pinned by test/test_ppm_reconstruction_rule.jl (CW84 eqs. 1.6-1.10 + cell-average parabola). Rust + Python siblings must match its per-grid Linf error to `tolerance.relative` and recover the rule's expected_min_order on the canonical smooth-periodic MMS sweep.

sub_stencil_cases.name: q_left_edge_offsets
sub_stencil_cases.notes: Sanity-check for sub_stencil dispatch: q_left_edge selectors are i-2..i+1 with coefficients (-1/12, 7/12, 7/12, -1/12).

sub_stencil_cases.name: q_right_edge_offsets
sub_stencil_cases.notes: q_right_edge selectors are i-1..i+2 with the same rational pattern.

output_kind_cases.name: parabola_xi_endpoints
output_kind_cases.notes: Parabola at xi=0 returns a_L; at xi=1 returns a_R, regardless of q_i (cell-average is preserved).

convergence_case.name: smooth_periodic_mms
convergence_case.notes: Reads tests/fixtures/ppm_reconstruction/{input,expected}.esm. Manufactured solution sin(2πx) + 0.3 cos(4πx) on [0,1] with periodic BC; cell averages computed analytically; PPM applied via the Rust evaluator's sub_stencil + output_kind dispatch. Verifies expected_min_order from expected.esm.
